019ee1be-3efd-7241-a5a6-a699e695b8cd.completed:
{
  "name": "skeptic",
  "claim": "Retained certification should remain blocked unless the artifact tree preserves raw host primitive evidence, not just runtime summaries.",
  "position": "conditional_block",
  "reasoning": [
    "r1-msg-001 frames this as adapter evidence for a real Codex swarm-expert spawn; a smoke can pass too easily if it only proves runtime bookkeeping.",
    "The retained tree must show spawn-order, host-step, wait-batches, collect-result, prompt-build, WAL, trace, and validation outputs with enough raw identifiers to prove the worker was real.",
    "Runtime-minted message ids are necessary but insufficient unless they can be traced back to preserved host ids and host-step artifacts."
  ],
  "proposals": [
    "Block certification if any required transport artifact is missing, empty, regenerated, or only summarized.",
    "Require an explicit mapping from runtime message ids to preserved Codex host ids or host step ids.",
    "Require validation commands to be retained with command text, exit status, and output path.",
    "Add a negative check that certification fails when collect-result or host-step evidence is removed.",
    "Keep the brief non-sensitive, but do not redact host primitive identifiers needed to verify transport."
  ],
  "references": [
    "r1-msg-001"
  ],
  "counterpoints": [
    "This is a minimal release smoke, so it does not need to prove every failure mode or multi-worker behavior.",
    "One real worker is enough if the artifact chain is raw, retained, and independently validated."
  ],
  "questions": [
    "Which exact files retain spawn-order, host-step, wait-batches, and collect-result artifacts?",
    "Are Codex host ids preserved verbatim, or only represented through runtime ids?",
    "Does adapter certification fail if a required transport artifact is deleted after the run?"
  ],
  "positionShift": true,
  "currentPosition": "Certification is blocked until raw host primitive evidence is retained and validated end to end.",
  "previousPosition": "No declared position before the moderator opening.",
  "shiftReason": "The moderator opening established that this smoke is specifically about retained runtime-owned adapter evidence for a real Codex swarm-expert spawn, which makes raw transport artifacts the core release risk.",
  "shiftTriggerIds": [
    "r1-msg-001"
  ]
}
